title: APF Paper 2 — The Structure of Admissible Physics: Non-Closure, Gauge Origin, Capacity Counting, and the 61-Type Partition
description:
42-page derivation deploying the quantum skeleton of Paper 1 to force the structure of the Standard Model from a single axiom: finite enforcement capacity (A1). Zero free parameters, zero empirical inputs, all results machine-verifiable via named check functions.

This repository is the Interactive Mathematical Appendix for Paper 2 of the Admissibility Physics Framework (APF). It contains the complete derivation chain from the Non-Closure Theorem through gauge template uniqueness, fermion content, capacity counting, and the cosmological density fractions.

Interactive resources (no install)

  • Derivation DAG — 33-node interactive theorem graph hosted on GitHub Pages; click any node to inspect dependencies and trace the shortest chain back to A1
  • Visual Storyboard — 12-panel illustrated walkthrough from the hydrogen atom to ΩΛ = 42/61; arrow-key navigation

What is derived in Paper 2

  • Non-Closure Theorem — two types proved: budget non-closure (Lncbudget, classical pigeonhole) and interference non-closure (Lncinterf, the quantum case with order-asymmetric surplus Δ12 ≠ Δ21)
  • Non-commutativity from A1 — Lirred (irreducibility from A1) + Schur's lemma → non-commuting enforcement algebra (LΔ→nc)
  • Cost function uniqueness — Cauchy's functional equation (1821) forces F(d) = d as the unique monotone additive cost function
  • Gauge template uniqueness — exhaustive classification of all 17 compact simple Lie algebras selects SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1) as the unique surviving product structure; capacity minimality selects Nc = 3
  • Fermion content — exhaustive scan of 4,680 chiral templates under seven sequential filters; unique survivor: Standard Model at 45 Weyl fermions
  • Ctotal = 61 — structural enforcement channels (not kinematic DOF): 45 fermion + 12 gauge + 4 Higgs
  • MECE partition 42 + 3 + 16 — two binary mechanism predicates (Q1, Q2) partition the 61 channels exhaustively: 42 vacuum, 3 baryonic, 16 dark
  • Cosmological density fractions — horizon equipartition (Lequip) gives ΩΛ = 42/61 = 0.6885, Ωm = 19/61 = 0.3115 with no empirical inputs

Three verification paths

  1. Zero-install Colab notebook — Open in Google Colab — annotated cells with inline LaTeX and executable code covering the full derivation chain from Lnc to ΩΛ = 42/61
  2. Local execution — clone the GitHub repository and run python run_checks.py (Python 3.8+, no pip install)
  3. Individual theorem inspection — every result traces to a named check function in gauge.py or core.py

Repository contents

  • apf/core.py — foundational lemmas (Lnc, Lirred, LΔ→nc, Lequip)
  • apf/gauge.py — gauge template uniqueness, fermion scan, capacity counting, cosmological fractions
  • apf/bank.py — theorem registry with run_all()
  • apf/L_Cauchy_uniqueness.py — cost function uniqueness proof
  • REVIEWERS_GUIDE.md — 9-section peer-review walkthrough addressing the non-closure chain, the Schur's lemma argument, the gauge classification, and six falsification surfaces
  • APF_Reviewer_Walkthrough_Paper2.ipynb — Colab notebook for referees

Accompanies the manuscript The Structure of Admissible Physics submitted to Foundations of Physics. Companion to Paper 1 (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18439200).
